name: Filesystem Baseline
description: Guards the host filesystem from the most dangerous operations: recursive root deletion, piping remote scripts to shell, block-device overwrites, and access to system credential files. Requires admin approval via access request for these; also scopes AI file-write access to the project directory and /tmp, requiring justification for writes elsewhere.

rules.name: Require access request for rm -rf on root or home
rules.description: rm -rf / and rm -rf ~ recursively delete the root or home directory. These are among the most destructive commands possible on a Unix system. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for piping remote scripts to shell
rules.description: curl URL | bash and wget URL | sh are a primary vector for supply-chain attacks and remote code execution. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request to overwrite block devices with dd
rules.description: dd if=... of=/dev/... writes directly to a block device (disk, partition, etc.), which can corrupt or wipe storage devices. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request to read sensitive system and credential files
rules.description: Reading /etc/shadow, /etc/passwd, SSH private keys, .aws/credentials, and .env files exposes credentials to AI context. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require justification for writing outside the project directory
rules.description: Write and Edit operations should be scoped to the current project directory or /tmp. Writes to any other path require a written justification explaining why the file needs to be created or modified outside the project.

rules.name: Warn on chmod 777
rules.description: chmod 777 grants world-read/write/execute permissions, which is a security misconfiguration in almost every context. Logs a warning without blocking.

rules.name: Warn on sudo usage
rules.description: sudo elevates privileges and can have broad system impact. Logs a warning when an AI agent uses sudo so the action can be reviewed.
